Beregost.position: half day's travel south on the Coast Way

connections.path: The Coast Way south - a well-maintained road through rolling farmland, patrolled but not entirely safe

Beregost.description: A prosperous market town where the ring of hammers on anvils echoes through cobblestone streets. The rose-gold spire of the Temple of Lathander catches the morning sun, while a half-dozen smithies compete for trade along Forge Row. Travelers find warm beds at the Burning Wizard inn, though lately the common room buzzes with dark rumors - bandits on the roads, iron mines gone silent, and stranger things stirring in the hills.

Baldur's Gate.position: several days north along the Coast Way
Baldur's Gate.description: The great city of Baldur's Gate dominates the northern coast - a sprawling metropolis of opportunity and peril behind massive grey walls. The Flaming Fist mercenaries keep order with iron discipline, while merchants, thieves, and adventurers crowd its famous Wide district.

The Coast Way Crossroads.position: half day's travel north toward Baldur's Gate

connections.path: The Coast Way north - wider road with more merchant traffic heading toward Baldur's Gate

The Coast Way Crossroads.description: A weathered waystation where the Coast Way meets the western trail. A bored Flaming Fist guard watches over a well, a hitching post, and a notice board thick with curling parchments - bounty postings, merchant requests, and warnings about gnoll raiders to the east. Wagon ruts scar the muddy ground in all directions. Merchants stop here to trade news before the final push to Baldur's Gate or the long road south.

Nashkel.position: two days south on the trade road
Nashkel.description: A dusty mining town known for its iron ore. Travelers speak of troubled times - the mines produce tainted metal and workers whisper of strange happenings underground. The mayor grows desperate for solutions.

connections.path: A rutted forest trail west - less traveled, winding through meadows before reaching the dark treeline

The Cloakwood Edge.description: Where farmland surrenders to ancient forest. A rough logging camp clusters at the treeline - canvas tents, stacked timber, and the smell of pine sap and woodsmoke. The loggers are a superstitious lot who refuse to work past midday, speaking of drums in the deep woods and shadows that move wrong. Beyond the stumps, the Cloakwood rises dark and tangled, its canopy blocking the sun even at noon.

Durlag's Tower.position: somewhere in the wilderness east of the Coast Way, location uncertain
Durlag's Tower.description: A cursed dwarven fortress. They say Durlag Trollkiller filled it with deadly traps to guard immense treasure. Few return.

The Friendly Arm Inn.position: Coast Way, between Baldur's Gate and Beregost

connections.path: The Coast Way south - a well-maintained road through rolling farmland, patrolled but not entirely safe
The Coast Way north - wider road with more merchant traffic heading toward Baldur's Gate
A rutted forest trail west - less traveled, winding through meadows before reaching the dark treeline

The Friendly Arm Inn.description: The ancient stone keep rises three stories against the darkening sky, its crenellated walls a reassuring bulwark against the dangers of the Coast Way. Torches flicker along the thirty-foot defensive walls that encircle the courtyard, where stabled horses stamp and snort in the evening chill. The massive iron-banded oak doors stand open, spilling warm amber light across the cobblestones. Inside, the common room fills the entire ground floor of the keep - a cavernous hall where a fire roars in a hearth large enough to roast a whole boar. The air hangs thick with the mingled scents of pipe smoke, spilled ale, and tonight's special: venison stew with rosemary and wild onions. Tallow candles and iron chandeliers cast dancing shadows across smoke-darkened beams and walls hung with faded tapestries depicting Sword Coast legends. The din of conversation rises and falls like waves - merchants haggling over morning departures, a weathered adventuring party comparing scars, pilgrims murmuring prayers to Tymora for safe roads. In the corner, a half-elf bard coaxes a melancholy ballad from her lute, her voice threading through the noise like silver. Serving staff weave between crowded tables bearing trenchers of bread and steaming bowls, while the clinking of ceramic mugs and the occasional burst of raucous laughter punctuate the evening's rhythm. Through the small windows, the last purple light of dusk fades to black, making the hearthfire seem all the warmer.

The Watcher's Ring.position: Deep in the Cloakwood, northwest of the logging camp

Thornhaven.position: At the foot of the Graypeak Mountains
Thornhaven.description: A bustling frontier town at the foot of the Graypeak Mountains. Stone buildings line cobbled streets, and the sound of hammers on anvils echoes from the craftsman's quarter. A wooden palisade surrounds the town, with guards posted at four gates. The central market square hosts traders from across the realm. The town has grown wealthy from iron mining but now faces declining yields.

The Rusty Blade Inn (2).description: A three-story building of weathered oak and stone on Thornhaven's eastern edge. The common room features a massive fireplace and walls decorated with ancient weapons. The inn's famous ale, 'Ironfoot's Revenge,' is known throughout the region. Guest rooms are simple but clean, and the basement holds Gareth's private collection of dwarven artifacts. Run by Gareth Ironfoot.

Tower of Stars.description: A tower rising from Thornhaven's northern district with a blue-tiled spire reaching toward the heavens. Ancient astronomical instruments crown the tower's summit. Inside, endless shelves of books and scrolls create a maze of knowledge. Magical lights float through the corridors, and the air smells of parchment and incense. Elara's private study occupies the highest floor.

The Old Mining Road.position: Through the foothills between Thornhaven and Echo Ridge
The Old Mining Road.description: A road winding through the foothills toward Echo Ridge. Once paved with stone, nature has reclaimed much of the path. Abandoned mining camps dot the route, their rotting structures home to wild animals. Recent goblin activity has left crude totems and warning signs along the way.

Graypeak Mountains.description: A mountain range where Echo Ridge Mine is located. Strange lights have been seen in these mountains recently, coinciding with rumors of the mine's rediscovery.

Echo Ridge Mine - Entrance.position: Carved into the mountainside at the end of the Old Mining Road
Echo Ridge Mine - Entrance.description: The entrance to Echo Ridge Mine carved into the mountainside. Massive stone doors bear ancient dwarven runes. Broken mining equipment litters the approach. Goblin sentries patrol from wooden watchtowers built among the ruins. Strange blue lights occasionally flicker from within the dark entrance.

Echo Ridge Mine - Upper Tunnels.position: Inside Echo Ridge Mine, above the Lower Depths
Echo Ridge Mine - Upper Tunnels.description: The upper tunnels now controlled by the Bloodfang goblin tribe under Chief Grimjaw. Goblins have built crude fortifications using mining carts and timber. The walls still bear tool marks from dwarven miners. Mushrooms growing in the darkness provide the goblins with food. The sound of pickaxes echoes from deeper tunnels where goblins search for treasure.

Echo Ridge Mine - Lower Depths.position: Deep within Echo Ridge Mine, below the Upper Tunnels
Echo Ridge Mine - Lower Depths.description: The unexplored lower depths since the mine's abandonment. Ancient dwarven architecture shows masterful stonework despite centuries of neglect. Strange whispers echo through the passages, and the temperature drops noticeably. Veins of a mysterious blue crystal pulse with inner light. The remains of dwarven miners lie where they fell during the goblin invasion.

The Forge of Thunderforge.position: Deepest chamber of Echo Ridge Mine
The Forge of Thunderforge.description: The legendary forge deep within Echo Ridge where the dwarves created magical weapons. The massive anvil remains intact, surrounded by scattered tools and half-finished works. The forge's fire has long gone cold, but magic still hums in the air. Ancient protective runes guard the chamber from evil. The Hammer of Thunderforge is rumored to be hidden here.

Mine Overseer's Office.description: An office within Echo Ridge Mine where the Amulet of the Mountain Lord is hidden.

Mine Storeroom.description: A storeroom within Echo Ridge Mine containing a few remaining Potions of Stone Giant Strength.

Thornhaven Temple.description: A temple of the Light in Thornhaven where Sister Alanna tends to the injured. A place of healing and spiritual guidance.

Basilisk Gate.position: Eastern gate of Baldur's Gate, main entry from the Coast Way. Flanked by Flaming Fist guards. Heavy merchant traffic.

The Tangles Safehouse.position: hidden room in the Tangles, Lower City, Baldur's Gate

The Tangles.position: thieves' quarter in Lower City, Baldur's Gate - Flaming Fist avoids after dark
